title: Explicit Photometric Sky Formula Features
summary: Test a fully specified photometric, sky, redshift, galactic, frequency, and rank feature block with simple balanced models.
feature_strategy: Build the feature set from explicit formulas, not from named external helpers. Start with alpha, delta, u, g, r, i, z, redshift, spectral_type, and galaxy_population. Add colors u_g=u-g, u_r=u-r, u_i=u-i, u_z=u-z, g_r=g-r, g_i=g-i, g_z=g-z, r_i=r-i, r_z=r-z. For mags=[u,g,r,i,z] and band index k=[0,1,2,3,4], add mag_sed_slope=(5*sum(k*mags)-10*sum(mags))/50, intercept=(sum(mags)-10*mag_sed_slope)/5, residuals=mags-(intercept+slope*k), mag_sed_resid_mean_abs, mag_sed_resid_std, mag_sed_d2_mean, mag_sed_d2_abs_mean, mag_sed_d2_std, mag_mean, mag_std, mag_range, band_min_mag, band_max_mag, mag_min_band, and mag_max_band. Convert alpha and delta to radians and add alpha_sin, alpha_cos, delta_sin, delta_cos, sky_x=cos(delta)*cos(alpha), sky_y=cos(delta)*sin(alpha), sky_z=sin(delta), sky_xy, sky_xz, sky_yz, and sky_x2_minus_y2. For harmonic orders 2,3,4,5,6,7,8 add alpha_sinK=sin(K*alpha), alpha_cosK=cos(K*alpha), delta_sinK=sin(K*delta), delta_cosK=cos(K*delta). Bin alpha modulo 360 into 24 equal bins and delta into 18 equal bins to create sky_alpha_bin_24, sky_delta_bin_18, sky_cell_bin=alpha_bin*19+delta_bin; also create sky_alpha_bin_48, sky_delta_bin_36, sky_cell_bin_48=alpha_bin*36+delta_bin. Add sky_alpha_bin_center_deg, sky_alpha_bin_offset_deg, sky_delta_bin_center_deg, and sky_delta_bin_offset_deg. On combined train+test covariates only, compute haversine nearest-neighbor distances over [delta_rad, alpha_rad] with n_neighbors=17; add sky_nn5_arcmin, sky_nn10_arcmin, sky_nn16_arcmin, sky_nn5_density=log1p(5/max(r5^2,1e-6)), sky_nn10_density=log1p(10/max(r10^2,1e-6)), sky_nn16_density=log1p(16/max(r16^2,1e-6)), and sky_nn_density_ratio_5_16. Add redshift_abs, redshift_sq, redshift_log_abs=log1p(abs(redshift)), redshift_signed_log_abs=sign(redshift)*log1p(abs(redshift)), train+test quantile redshift_bin_20, spectral_type__redshift_bin_20, and galaxy_population__redshift_bin_20. Convert equatorial alpha/delta to galactic coordinates using the rotation matrix [-0.0548755604,-0.8734370902,-0.4838350155;0.4941094279,-0.44482963,0.7469822445;-0.8676661490,-0.1980763734,0.4559837762], then add galactic_l, galactic_b, galactic_l_sin, galactic_l_cos, galactic_b_sin, galactic_b_cos, galactic_b_abs, galactic_l_bin_24, galactic_b_bin_12, galactic_cell_bin=l_bin*12+b_bin, spectral_type__galactic_cell_bin, and galaxy_population__galactic_cell_bin. Add categorical crosses spectral_type__galaxy_population, spectral_type__band_min_band, galaxy_population__band_max_band, spectral_type__sky_alpha_bin_24, galaxy_population__sky_alpha_bin_24, spectral_type__sky_cell_bin, spectral_type__sky_alpha_bin_48, galaxy_population__sky_alpha_bin_48, spectral_type__sky_cell_bin_48, and spectral_type__galaxy_population__sky_delta_bin_18. For sky/redshift/galactic bin columns add train+test covariate-only normalized frequency columns named <column>_freq. For u, g, r, i, z, and redshift add train+test percentile rank columns u_qrank, g_qrank, r_qrank, i_qrank, z_qrank, and redshift_qrank using average rank. Do not add model blending, calibration, stacking, or multiple variants per model family.
baseline_model_panel: Use one CatBoost multiclass model with auto_class_weights="Balanced"; one XGBoost multiclass model with fold-specific sample_weight=compute_sample_weight(class_weight="balanced", y=y_train) passed to fit; one LightGBM multiclass model with class_weight="balanced"; and optionally one HistGradientBoostingClassifier sanity baseline using fold-specific balanced sample weights if supported. Use one configuration per family only.
model_panel_rationale: The feature block is the experiment. A simple one-model-per-family panel is enough to measure whether the explicit representation carries signal and which basic tree family uses it best.
validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on competition train rows and score balanced accuracy. Train+test frequency, bin, rank, and neighbor-density features may use only covariates, never class labels or predictions. If auxiliary SDSS17 rows are used, train on them only inside each fold and report the primary score only on competition validation rows.
materialization_hint: Implement the formulas written in feature_strategy directly. Do not reference external helper names. Fit the simple independent model panel, log per-model fold scores and runtime, and select the best single model by mean CV balanced accuracy. Exclude variant weighting, affine calibration, class-wise blend tuning, stacking, temperature scaling, and hyperparameter search.
expected_signal: A useful result should put the best single simple model near the strong single-expert range from the source family, roughly mid-to-high 0.967 CV balanced accuracy, with feature importance emphasizing color/profile, redshift, sky/galactic, frequency, rank, and neighbor-density blocks.
risk: The feature block is broad and may be slower than a minimal root. Train+test covariate features are transductive, so the run must log that they use no labels. The hypothesis intentionally tests features, so adding heavy ensembles or calibration would make the result hard to interpret.
rationale:
Feature family: explicit_photometric_sky_formula_features
Feature strategy: implement the exact formulas listed in feature_strategy: color differences; ugriz SED slope/residual/second-difference/aggregate/extrema features; alpha/delta trigonometric, 3D, interaction, harmonic, bin, offset, and neighbor-density features; redshift transforms and redshift-category interactions; galactic coordinate/bin/interaction features; categorical crosses; train+test covariate-only frequencies; and percentile ranks for u,g,r,i,z,redshift.
Baseline model panel: CatBoost with auto_class_weights="Balanced"; XGBoost with fold-specific sample_weight=compute_sample_weight(class_weight="balanced", y=y_train); LightGBM with class_weight="balanced"; optional HistGB sanity baseline with balanced sample weights if supported. No variants, no stacking, no class-wise blending, no affine calibration, no temperature scaling, no hyperparameter search.
Validation strategy: 5-fold StratifiedKFold on competition rows with balanced accuracy. The hypothesis score should be the best single model's mean CV=5 result.
implementation_hint: Build the feature set from explicit formulas, not from named external helpers. Start with alpha, delta, u, g, r, i, z, redshift, spectral_type, and galaxy_population. Add colors u_g=u-g, u_r=u-r, u_i=u-i, u_z=u-z, g_r=g-r, g_i=g-i, g_z=g-z, r_i=r-i, r_z=r-z. For mags=[u,g,r,i,z] and band index k=[0,1,2,3,4], add mag_sed_slope=(5*sum(k*mags)-10*sum(mags))/50, intercept=(sum(mags)-10*mag_sed_slope)/5, residuals=mags-(intercept+slope*k), mag_sed_resid_mean_abs, mag_sed_resid_std, mag_sed_d2_mean, mag_sed_d2_abs_mean, mag_sed_d2_std, mag_mean, mag_std, mag_range, band_min_mag, band_max_mag, mag_min_band, and mag_max_band. Convert alpha and delta to radians and add alpha_sin, alpha_cos, delta_sin, delta_cos, sky_x=cos(delta)*cos(alpha), sky_y=cos(delta)*sin(alpha), sky_z=sin(delta), sky_xy, sky_xz, sky_yz, and sky_x2_minus_y2. For harmonic orders 2,3,4,5,6,7,8 add alpha_sinK=sin(K*alpha), alpha_cosK=cos(K*alpha), delta_sinK=sin(K*delta), delta_cosK=cos(K*delta). Bin alpha modulo 360 into 24 equal bins and delta into 18 equal bins to create sky_alpha_bin_24, sky_delta_bin_18, sky_cell_bin=alpha_bin*19+delta_bin; also create sky_alpha_bin_48, sky_delta_bin_36, sky_cell_bin_48=alpha_bin*36+delta_bin. Add sky_alpha_bin_center_deg, sky_alpha_bin_offset_deg, sky_delta_bin_center_deg, and sky_delta_bin_offset_deg. On combined train+test covariates only, compute haversine nearest-neighbor distances over [delta_rad, alpha_rad] with n_neighbors=17; add sky_nn5_arcmin, sky_nn10_arcmin, sky_nn16_arcmin, sky_nn5_density=log1p(5/max(r5^2,1e-6)), sky_nn10_density=log1p(10/max(r10^2,1e-6)), sky_nn16_density=log1p(16/max(r16^2,1e-6)), and sky_nn_density_ratio_5_16. Add redshift_abs, redshift_sq, redshift_log_abs=log1p(abs(redshift)), redshift_signed_log_abs=sign(redshift)*log1p(abs(redshift)), train+test quantile redshift_bin_20, spectral_type__redshift_bin_20, and galaxy_population__redshift_bin_20. Convert equatorial alpha/delta to galactic coordinates using the rotation matrix [-0.0548755604,-0.8734370902,-0.4838350155;0.4941094279,-0.44482963,0.7469822445;-0.8676661490,-0.1980763734,0.4559837762], then add galactic_l, galactic_b, galactic_l_sin, galactic_l_cos, galactic_b_sin, galactic_b_cos, galactic_b_abs, galactic_l_bin_24, galactic_b_bin_12, galactic_cell_bin=l_bin*12+b_bin, spectral_type__galactic_cell_bin, and galaxy_population__galactic_cell_bin. Add categorical crosses spectral_type__galaxy_population, spectral_type__band_min_band, galaxy_population__band_max_band, spectral_type__sky_alpha_bin_24, galaxy_population__sky_alpha_bin_24, spectral_type__sky_cell_bin, spectral_type__sky_alpha_bin_48, galaxy_population__sky_alpha_bin_48, spectral_type__sky_cell_bin_48, and spectral_type__galaxy_population__sky_delta_bin_18. For sky/redshift/galactic bin columns add train+test covariate-only normalized frequency columns named <column>_freq. For u, g, r, i, z, and redshift add train+test percentile rank columns u_qrank, g_qrank, r_qrank, i_qrank, z_qrank, and redshift_qrank using average rank. Do not add model blending, calibration, stacking, or multiple variants per model family. Use class balancing explicitly: CatBoost auto_class_weights="Balanced"; XGBoost fold-specific sample_weight=compute_sample_weight(class_weight="balanced", y=y_train); LightGBM class_weight="balanced"; optional HistGB balanced sample weights if supported. Fit one CatBoost, one XGBoost, one LightGBM, and optional HistGB; choose the best single model by mean 5-fold balanced accuracy. Do not implement variant ensembles, calibration, stacking, temperature scaling, or blend-weight search.
expected_effect: If the explicit feature block is useful, a single boosted tree should approach the strong single-expert range from the source family. If it does not, then the earlier strong result likely depended more on ensemble/calibration than on the feature representation.
